content.problemStatement:
You are working on a program that needs to store information about students. \
Since students can have different types of information, you decide to use a union to represent their data.

The code in the IDE does the following
- Declare a union named StudentInfo with the following members:
   - rollNumber of type int
   - name of type char[50]
   - marks of type float
- Accept user input consisting of 2 lines
  - 1st line is the value 1 / 2 / 3 indicating `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
  - 2nd line has the `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
- Use a switch statement to identify the type of information entered and store it in the appropriate member of the union.
- Output the student's information.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq N \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ over all testcases does not exceed $2\cdot 10^3$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of user inputs.
- Each user input consists of 2 lines of input 
  - 1st line is the value 1 / 2 / 3 indicating `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
  - 2nd line has the `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
content.outputFormat: Output the student's information as per the sample output shown below.
